response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills with C/systems experience, this role has two critical mismatches: the job explicitly requires native-level Japanese language proficiency (the candidate lists English, Romanian, and German fluently), and the position demands embedded networking/security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, kernel analysis, network security) that is absent from the resume. Recent experience (2015–present) focuses on web, full-stack, and cloud development rather than embedded firmware.
strengths: Proven full-stack engineering capability across multiple domains with demonstrated systems-level thinking through low-latency application work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests
Direct C/C++ and embedded systems experience from National Instruments, plus infrastructure expertise (Linux, CI/CD, Docker) showing ability to navigate complex technical environments
Strong mentorship record and teaching background (workshops, code katas, courses) demonstrating communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams
concerns: Missing explicit required qualification: native-level Japanese language proficiency (candidate has only English, Romanian, and German listed as fluent languages)
No visible experience with embedded networking/security technologies essential for the role (MACsec, 802.1X, network switch operation, encryption protocols, device tamper detection)
Embedded systems experience is 10+ years old (last role 2014–2015); entire recent career (2015–present) focused on web/cloud development, not embedded firmware or security

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking and security domain expertise
General C programming and systems engineering foundation
